title: Add passwd validate and generate steps
body:

comments.body:
FYI: if this is is about https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23468 I have seen this only fails twice over the span of months so you are not likely to hit soon in PRs I think.

It might make sense to copy the function and run it locally to generate a bunch of passwords, just from a quick a look a at the function it generates random bytes which to me seems like there is actually no guarantee that it contains a upper case and lower case and a number. So I think he first check would be to see what is actually the policy used here and then making sure the generate follow that policy.
should we merge this so if it fails, we have a possible outcome ?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *inknos* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

sounds like @inknos has a theory, so hold off on merge ... 

also @inknos, if you force push, mind changing the title of the PR and giving a decent commit message?

> if you force push, mind changing the title of the PR and giving a decent commit message?

Sure it was just a placeholder. I'll be nice with the commit
LGTM, but i dont speak powershell well ... @ashley-cui @l0rd mind reviewing ?
